proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 36.1% on enzalutamide vs 15.9% off; OR 2.99, chi-square p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have lower objective_response rate than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0 patients.
Patients with msi_high=1 have lower objective_response rate than msi_high=0 patients.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than psma_high=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 14.97% in BRCA2+ vs 25.03% BRCA2 WT; difference -10.05 pp.
ORR 15.99% in AR-V7+ vs 26.04% AR-V7-; difference -10.05 pp.
ORR 17.60% in MSI-high vs 24.22% MSS; difference -6.62 pp.
ORR 23.86% PSMA-high vs 24.26% PSMA-low; difference -0.40 pp, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher gleason_score is associated with lower objective_response.
Patients with mcrpc=1 have lower objective_response rate than mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive) patients.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have lower objective_response than visceral_mets=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: OR 1.0002 per year, p=0.82; no association.
ORR essentially flat across Gleason 6-10 (~24%); OR 1.005 per unit, p=0.61.
ORR 34.6% non-mCRPC vs 15.4% mCRPC; difference -19.2 pp.
ORR 24.0% with visceral mets vs 24.0% without; no association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher psa_ng_ml (log scale) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Routine chemistry labs (ast, alt, total_bilirubin, creatinine, bun, sodium, potassium, calcium) are associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: OR 0.531 per log10 unit; ORR 31.8% in lowest PSA quartile vs 15.3% in highest.
OR 1.10 per g/dL; ORR rises from 23.1% (Q1) to 25.1% (Q4).
OR 0.9999 per U/L, p=0.68; ORR flat across quartiles.
OR 1.0002 per U/L, p=0.34; no signal.
OR 0.996 per g/dL, p=0.51; flat by quartile.

analyses.code: univariate logits for ast, alt, total_bilirubin, creatinine, bun, sodium, potassium, calcium
analyses.result_summary: All p>0.13; no chemistry lab is meaningfully associated with ORR univariately.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: OR 0.997 per unit, p=0.61; quartile ORRs flat (~24%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable logistic model for objective_response, treatment_enzalutamide remains a strong positive predictor; mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, ecog_ps, log_psa, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and crp_mg_l remain negative; albumin_g_dl remains positive; the other treatments do not contribute.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs: enza 3.32 (p<1e-300), mcrpc 0.34, brca2 0.48, arv7 0.50, msi 0.65, ecog 0.72, log_psa 0.84, weight_loss 0.96, crp 0.99 (all p<1e-4); albumin 1.14 (p<1e-4). Abi/doc/ola/lu/pem ORs all 0.97-1.03, none significant. Age, gleason, visceral, ldh, alp, hgb, nlr, psma_high all non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated (smaller or absent) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 compared with brca2_mutation=0.
The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 compared with ar_v7_positive=0.
The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in patients with msi_high=1 compared with msi_high=0.
The objective_response effect of treatment_olaparib is enhanced (positive) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 versus brca2_mutation=0.
The objective_response effect of treatment_pembrolizumab is enhanced in patients with msi_high=1 versus msi_high=0.
The objective_response effect of treatment_lu177_psma is enhanced in patients with psma_high=1 versus psma_high=0.

analyses.result_summary: Enza ORR=15.3% in BRCA2+ vs 38.5% in BRCA2 WT; enza ORR=14.7% off in BRCA2+ (no benefit). Interaction logOR=-1.14, p=3.3e-42.
Enza ORR=16.5% in AR-V7+ vs 41.1% in AR-V7-; off-enza ORR ~16% regardless. Interaction logOR=-1.24, p=1.4e-93.
Enza ORR=16.2% in MSI-high vs 36.7% in MSS; interaction logOR=-1.28, p=1.8e-19.
Olaparib ORR 12.1% in BRCA2+ vs 25.3% BRCA2 WT; in BRCA2+ patients olaparib does NOT improve response (12.1% on vs 15.3% off). Interaction logOR=-0.29, p=0.045 (refutes expected enhancement).
MSI-high+pembro n=79: ORR 17.7% on vs 17.6% off; interaction p=0.96. No detectable enhancement.
PSMA-high+lu177 ORR 23.8% vs PSMA-high off 23.9%; interaction p=0.36. No enhancement in PSMA-high.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in mcrpc=1 patients compared with mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive) patients.
The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide differs by visceral_mets status.
The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide differs by ecog_ps.
The objective_response effect of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, or pembrolizumab is modified by mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ecog_ps.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + mcrpc + enza:mcrpc); stratified ORR
analyses.result_summary: Non-mCRPC: enza ORR 61.0% vs off 16.9% (diff +44.0 pp). mCRPC: enza ORR 15.8% vs off 15.0% (diff +0.8 pp). Interaction logOR=-1.98, p<1e-300.
No interaction: logOR=-0.009, p=0.86.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + ecog + enza:ecog); stratified ORR by ECOG
analyses.result_summary: Enza diff +20.7 pp at ECOG 0, +20.1 at ECOG 1, +19.8 at ECOG 2 (similar). Interaction logOR=+0.14 p=1.6e-5 (small magnitude — driven by lower baseline at higher ECOG making the OR scale slightly larger).

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ tx + clin + tx:clin) for each non-enza treatment x mcrpc/visceral/ecog
analyses.result_summary: Of 15 tested interactions, only lu177:visceral reaches p<0.05 (logOR=-0.19, p=0.010), with no clinically relevant magnitude. None survive multiple-testing correction; nothing approaches the enza signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in patients with high psa_ng_ml (log scale).
The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is modified by albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, or alkaline_phosphatase_u_l.

analyses.result_summary: Univariately, interaction logOR=-0.97, p=2.3e-127 (enza benefit appears smaller at high PSA).

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + lab + enza:lab) for each of {albumin, weight_loss, crp, ldh, hgb, alp, ast, alt, total_bili, creatinine, bun, sodium, potassium, calcium}
analyses.result_summary: Only enza:albumin (p=0.06), enza:creatinine (p=0.04), and enza:ecog (p=2e-5) approached/passed p<0.05; all magnitudes small. No lab modifier rivals the biomarker/mCRPC signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide grows progressively as we restrict to non-mCRPC, then additionally to AR-V7-, then BRCA2 wild-type, then MSI stable. The fully restricted subgroup shows the largest treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: All-comers: +20.2 pp. Non-mCRPC: +44.0 pp. Non-mCRPC + AR-V7-: +54.6 pp. Non-mCRPC + AR-V7- + BRCA2 WT: +60.7 pp. Non-mCRPC + AR-V7- + BRCA2 WT + MSI stable: +62.6 pp (enza ORR 79.8% vs off 17.2%). Cascade monotonic and large.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each non-enzalutamide treatment (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) shows a meaningful objective_response benefit in at least one clinically motivated subgroup (e.g., olaparib in BRCA2+/mCRPC, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, lu177 in PSMA-high).

analyses.code: Stratified ORR for each non-enza treatment in clinically plausible subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA2+ mCRPC: ORR 11.1% on vs 15.2% off (diff -4.1 pp). Pembrolizumab in MSI-high mCRPC: 8.9% vs 16.1% (diff -7.2 pp; MSI-high non-mCRPC n=34 is +10 pp but tiny). Lu177 in PSMA-high mCRPC: 15.9% vs 15.0% (diff +0.9 pp). Abiraterone in non-mCRPC AR-V7- BRCA2 WT MSI stable: 41.6% vs 42.8% (diff -1.2 pp). Docetaxel in mCRPC + visceral: 15.9% vs 15.2% (+0.7 pp). No non-enza treatment shows a meaningful benefit in any clinically motivated subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across an exhaustive single-feature interaction screen for treatment_enzalutamide, the strongest objective_response effect modifiers (in descending magnitude) are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, log_psa, and ecog_ps; other features show no meaningful interaction.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + f + enza:f) for each f in 25 features
analyses.result_summary: Top interaction p-values for enza: mcrpc (0.0), log_psa (2e-127), ar_v7 (1e-93), brca2 (3e-42), msi (2e-19), ecog (2e-5). All others p>0.03. The four top biomarker/clinical modifiers all have logOR magnitudes >1 (large attenuation); log_psa magnitude ~1 but driven mostly by mCRPC confounding (see iter 13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the non-mCRPC subgroup, log_psa no longer modifies the objective_response effect of treatment_enzalutamide; the apparent log_psa interaction in iter 12 was driven by the strong correlation between mcrpc=1 and elevated PSA.
Within the non-mCRPC subgroup, the only remaining strong objective_response modifiers of treatment_enzalutamide are ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and msi_high.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + log_psa + enza:log_psa) restricted to mcrpc=0
analyses.result_summary: Within non-mCRPC, enza:log_psa interaction logOR=-0.027, p=0.72 (null). Stratified ORR: enza diff is +44.8 pp (Q1 PSA), +44.3 pp (Q2), +43.8 pp (Q3), +43.3 pp (Q4) — essentially flat. PSA is NOT an enza effect modifier; the prior signal was confounding.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + f + enza:f) for each f, restricted to mcrpc=0
analyses.result_summary: Within non-mCRPC, top interactions: ar_v7 logOR=-2.41 p=4e-163; brca2 logOR=-2.34 p=1e-78; msi logOR=-2.17 p=3e-27; psma_high logOR=-0.14 p=0.03 (small). All others p>0.06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four conditions (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0, brca2_mutation=0, msi_high=0) is necessary for the enzalutamide objective_response effect: removing any one condition (knock-in of an unfavorable marker) collapses the treatment effect to ~0.

analyses.code: Risk-difference of enza in subgroups violating exactly one of the 4 conditions (others kept favorable)
analyses.result_summary: Knock-in mCRPC (others favorable): diff +0.7 pp (p=0.17). Knock-in AR-V7+ (others favorable): diff +2.1 pp (p=0.09). Knock-in BRCA2+ (others favorable): diff +0.4 pp (p=0.88). Knock-in MSI-high (others favorable): diff -2.5 pp (p=0.58). Each knock-in eliminates the enza response; all four conditions are necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0), treatment_enzalutamide is associated with a very large odds-ratio increase in objective_response (OR >> 5). In the complement subgroup, the enzalutamide OR is approximately 1.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza) within favorable subgroup; same in complement
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable (n=15,681): enza ORR 79.8% vs off 17.2%, OR 18.98, p<1e-300. Within unfavorable (n=34,319): enza ORR 16.1% vs off 15.3%, OR 1.06, p=0.054.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive single-feature interaction screen for each non-enzalutamide treatment yields no replicable objective_response modifier; any p<0.05 hits are small in magnitude, in tiny subgroups, or in directions opposite to clinical expectation, consistent with multiple-testing noise.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ tx + f + tx:f) for each non-enza tx in {abi, doc, ola, lu, pem} crossed with 25 features (125 tests)
analyses.result_summary: Hits p<0.05: abiraterone:brca2 (logOR +0.19, p=0.031); docetaxel:sodium (-0.017, p=0.023), :alt (-0.004, p=0.028); olaparib:bun (-0.015, p=0.031), :brca2 (-0.29, p=0.045 — opposite of expected direction); lu177:visceral (-0.19, p=0.010), :creatinine (+0.19, p=0.046); pembrolizumab:gleason (+0.10, p=0.033). At alpha=0.05/125=4e-4, none survive. None have meaningful effect-size implications.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the unfavorable subgroup (any of mcrpc=1, brca2_mutation=1, ar_v7_positive=1, or msi_high=1), the conditional ORR (off any treatment) is dominated by mcrpc status, BRCA2 status, AR-V7 status, and MSI status; no treatment improves response.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit on objective_response within the unfavorable subgroup; stratified ORR by combination of unfavorable markers, with and without each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Across all 14 combinations of {mcrpc, brca2, ar_v7, msi} that include at least one unfavorable marker, ORRs hover at 9-25% with or without enzalutamide; differences are in the range -7 pp to +9 pp, none robustly significant. The unfavorable set is essentially treatment-non-responsive in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a joint logistic model that simultaneously includes enzalutamide × {mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high} interactions and adjusts for log_psa, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, and psma_high, all four enzalutamide interactions remain large and highly significant.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + 4 modifiers + 6 covariates + 4 enza:modifier interactions)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enza × modifier logORs: enza:mcrpc -2.23 (p<1e-300), enza:brca2 -1.55 (p<1e-300), enza:ar_v7 -1.65 (p<1e-300), enza:msi -1.67 (p<1e-300). Main enza logOR in absence of modifiers = +2.63 (OR 13.8). All four interactions independently large and not collinear.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite 'favorable' indicator (mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0; ~31% of cohort) interacts with treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response with very large magnitude.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR = +2.89 (OR 17.91), p<1e-300. ORR 79.8% (favorable+enza), 17.2% (favorable, no enza), 16.1% (unfavorable+enza), 15.3% (unfavorable, no enza). The composite indicator captures essentially all of the enzalutamide treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each non-enzalutamide treatment, an exhaustive search across 22 candidate single- and pairwise-defined subgroups identifies no subgroup with a meaningful (>=5 pp) increase in objective_response on the treatment.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR for each non-enza tx across 22 candidate subgroups (favorable, unfavorable, single-marker, pairwise marker combos, ECOG strata, visceral strata)
analyses.result_summary: No non-enzalutamide treatment shows a +5 pp or larger ORR difference in any candidate subgroup. The only |diff|>=5 pp hits are NEGATIVE (e.g., olaparib in BRCA2+ mCRPC -4.1 pp; pembrolizumab in MSI-high mCRPC -7.2 pp; PSMA-high MSI-high subgroups -6 to -7 pp), all in small subgroups likely reflecting noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, adding any of the other treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) to enzalutamide does not further improve objective_response over enzalutamide alone.

analyses.code: Within favorable & on tx_X, compare ORR with vs without tx_Y for each pair of treatments
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide added to other backbone (abiraterone, docetaxel) yields the same +62 pp ORR increment as enzalutamide alone, so the enzalutamide effect is not modified by background treatment. No other pairing shows meaningful improvement; lu177+pembrolizumab actually shows -7 pp combined vs lu177 alone (n=102, exploratory only).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no continuous lab feature meaningfully modifies the enzalutamide objective_response benefit.

analyses.code: logit(OR ~ enza + f + enza:f) within favorable, for each f in 13 features
analyses.result_summary: Smallest within-favorable interaction p-values: psma_high (logOR -0.25, p=0.004), hemoglobin (+0.044, p=0.058), ecog (-0.113, p=0.069). All small magnitude; the favorable-subgroup enza effect is robust (~+62 pp) regardless.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the enzalutamide objective_response benefit is consistent across ECOG strata, visceral_mets strata, and PSA tertiles.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0/1/2: +64.6 / +62.2 / +59.0 pp. Visceral 0/1: +62.6 / +62.3 pp. PSA tertile low/mid/high: +63.5 / +61.7 / +62.5 pp. Treatment effect is highly robust across these strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfavorable patients, every combination of one or more unfavorable markers (mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high) is associated with a near-null enzalutamide objective_response effect (|diff| <= 10 pp), with no combination identifying a residual responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: Cross-tab ORR by violation pattern x enza
analyses.result_summary: Across all 14 unfavorable combinations (single-, double-, triple-, quadruple-marker), enza-vs-no-enza ORR differences range from -9 pp to +9 pp; no large combinations have n>50 with diff > 5 pp. The treatment-responsive subgroup is bounded exactly by the 4-condition favorable definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final consolidated objective_response heterogeneity hypothesis: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive objective_response benefit (ORR rises from ~17% off-enzalutamide to ~80% on-enzalutamide; risk-difference +63 pp; OR ~19) confined to the subgroup defined simultaneously by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the treatment effect is null (OR ~1.06, p=0.054). No other treatment in the panel (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab) shows a clinically or statistically meaningful objective_response effect in any feature-defined subgroup tested.

analyses.code: Final pooled stratified comparison and joint interaction model
analyses.result_summary: Favorable enza+: ORR 79.8% (n=6325). Favorable enza-: ORR 17.2% (n=9356). Unfavorable enza+: ORR 16.1% (n=13,751). Unfavorable enza-: ORR 15.3% (n=20,568). Composite favorable x enza interaction OR=17.9 (p<1e-300). The favorable subgroup is necessary AND sufficient: knockout of any single condition collapses the effect (iter 14); joint model retains all four interaction terms with logOR < -1.5 (iter 18). Other-treatment subgroup search returned no positive results (iters 11, 16, 20).
